heading: Counseling, training, and mental health services for immediate family members and caregivers
label: 1782 Counseling, training, and mental health services for immediate family members and caregivers
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 1782 was renumbered .section 3682 of this title

Amendments2010—, inserted “and caregivers” after “members” in section catchline.Pub. L. 111–163, § 103(b)Subsec. (c)(2), (3). , added par. (2) and redesignated former par. (2) as (3).Pub. L. 111–163, § 103(a)2008—Subsec. (a). , inserted “marriage and family counseling,” after “professional counseling,”.Pub. L. 110–387, § 301(a)(2)(A)Subsec. (b). , inserted “marriage and family counseling,” after “professional counseling,” and substituted period at end for “if—Pub. L. 110–387, § 301(a)(2)(B)“(1) those services were initiated during the veteran’s hospitalization; and“(2) the continued provision of those services on an outpatient basis is essential to permit the discharge of the veteran from the hospital.”

children.heading: .—Counseling for Family Members of Veterans Receiving Service-Connected Treatment
children.label: a .—Counseling for Family Members of Veterans Receiving Service-Connected Treatment
children.text: In the case of a veteran who is receiving treatment for a service-connected disability pursuant to paragraph (1) or (2) of , the Secretary shall provide to individuals described in subsection (c) such consultation, professional counseling, marriage and family counseling, training, and mental health services as are necessary in connection with that treatment.section 1710(a) of this title

children.heading: .—Counseling for Family Members of Veterans Receiving Non-Service-Connected Treatment
children.label: b .—Counseling for Family Members of Veterans Receiving Non-Service-Connected Treatment
children.text: In the case of a veteran who is eligible to receive treatment for a non-service-connected disability under the conditions described in paragraph (1), (2), or (3) of , the Secretary may, in the discretion of the Secretary, provide to individuals described in subsection (c) such consultation, professional counseling, marriage and family counseling, training, and mental health services as are necessary in connection with that treatment.section 1710(a) of this title
the members of the immediate family or the legal guardian of a veteran;
a family caregiver of an eligible veteran or a caregiver of a covered veteran (as those terms are defined in ); orsection 1720G of this title
the individual in whose household such veteran certifies an intention to live.
A veteran who is receiving care for a service-connected disability.
A dependent or survivor receiving care under the last sentence of .section 1783(b) of this title
